_comment: Drives BOTH sides of the validator-parity harness (backend#828 P3). cli_verdict = what the Go preflight must decide; ingestor_verdict = what the REAL Python validators decide (pinned by goldens.json, regenerated via scripts/gen-validator-goldens.py). The two may deliberately diverge only where the note explains why (e.g. failures the ingestor only surfaces at transfer time, which the CLI previews anyway).

cases.name: tabular-dup-header
cases.note: DataValidator preflight probe + read-time twin (cli#73a)

cases.name: tabular-bom
cases.note: DELIBERATE divergence (surfaced by the #348 schema re-sync, which pinned the ref past data-ingestors #338): at THIS pinned ref the in-cluster tabular schema probe now strips the BOM, so the ingestor accepts. But the DEPLOYED ingestor (v0.5.7) predates #338 and still falsely rejects a BOM'd tabular CSV post-upload, so the CLI keeps previewing that rejection (CheckTabularBOM, cli#71). Drop this divergence once #338 ships to prod. Flagged for follow-up.

cases.name: tabular-header-only
cases.note: IngestableRecordsValidator (cli#73b) (residual masking: a header-only CSV also has 0 label classes, so diversity rejects too — unavoidable for this shape)

cases.name: tabular-label-missing
cases.note: DELIBERATE divergence: the ingestor's preflight validators pass — the failure only fires at READ time (full-schema check, after table creation, orphaning it). The CLI previews the read-time rule (cli#69).

cases.name: imgc-bom-labels
cases.note: pandas strips the BOM in-cluster — the CLI must NOT reject what the cluster accepts (cli#71 parity)

cases.name: imgc-label-missing
cases.note: LabelColumnValidator (cli#69)

cases.name: imgc-label-case
cases.note: the ingestor's _match_column is case-insensitive + trimmed — the CLI must be exactly as loose

cases.name: imgc-zero-byte
cases.note: ImageResolutionValidator: empty file (cli#72b)

cases.name: imgc-corrupt
cases.note: ImageResolutionValidator: not a valid image (cli#72b)

cases.name: imgc-res-mismatch
cases.note: ImageResolutionValidator: exact equality, no resize (cli#72c)

cases.name: imgc-too-small
cases.note: DELIBERATE divergence (deployment parity, #348): 16x16 images are below the 32x32 default floor, so the pinned ingestor (develop, di#348/#356) rejects. But the CLI must NOT block by default — the DEPLOYED ingestor (v0.5.7/v0.6.0) has NO floor and ingests these fine, so a default local reject would block an ingest the live cluster accepts (the inverse of tabular-bom, whose reject mirrors a real deployed rejection). The CLI previews the floor ONLY when --min-size is set (see imgc-min-size-override). Flip this to reject and default the preview floor once di#348 ships to prod. Flagged for follow-up.

cases.name: imgc-min-size-override
cases.note: the --min-size override, cross-checked end-to-end (#348; closes the parity gap where min_size was representable on neither side). 24x40 images (W,H) with an explicit min_size [16,32] pass on BOTH sides — but the DEFAULT 32x32 floor would reject them (W=24<32), so this proves the override is actually read (not silently the default) and its [W,H] orientation is honored (a swapped [32,16] would reject on W=24<32). target_size matches the images, so the floor override is the sole discriminator.

cases.name: imgc-header-only
cases.note: IngestableRecordsValidator (cli#73b) (residual masking: a header-only CSV also has 0 label classes, so diversity rejects too — unavoidable for this shape)

cases.name: imgc-missing-file
cases.note: DELIBERATE divergence: the ingestor's preflight passes — the row becomes a failed record at TRANSFER time ('completed with failures', exit 9, post-upload). The CLI previews the transfer fate (cli#72a).

cases.name: imgc-label-uniform
cases.note: LabelDiversityValidator: classification needs >=2 distinct labels — discovered BY this harness's first run (was not in any ticket)

cases.name: imgc-nonsquare
cases.note: non-square [W,H]=[64,32] (both sides >= the 32x32 floor): pins the target_size ORIENTATION end-to-end — an [H,W] swap on emit (the pre-P3 bug) flips this to reject in-cluster

cases.name: imgc-nonsquare-swapped
cases.note: the same 64×32 images with target [32,64]: both sides must reject — proves the comparison is orientation-sensitive, not shape-normalized

cases.name: imgc-dup-header
cases.note: DELIBERATE divergence: image categories have no DataValidator, so the ingestor's preflight accepts dup headers — the rejection fires at READ time (after table creation, orphaning it). The CLI previews the read-time rule.

cases.name: imgc-empty-label
cases.note: empty-string label IS a class for image categories (keep_default_na=False): the CLI must not be stricter

cases.name: imgc-dotted-stem
cases.note: row 'photo.2024' resolves to photo.2024.jpg via _has_extension (dotted stems are not extensions) — the CLI's cross-check must mirror, not reject

cases.name: tabular-na-labels
cases.note: 'NA' drops to NaN for schema-typed labels (coercion NA_SENTINELS) → 1 distinct class in-cluster; the CLI mirrors the sentinel drop

cases.name: tabular-nonutf8
cases.note: check_csv_encoding runs before ANY validator in-cluster; Latin-1 exports must fail locally, not post-upload

cases.name: text-clf-ok
cases.note: pins the text-family dispatch

cases.name: tabular-varchar-numeric-labels
cases.note: labels '1' vs '1.0' under a VARCHAR label: the ingestor pins dtype=str (no numeric collapse) so they are 2 classes — pins #152's schema-type-aware collapse (the earlier blanket collapse falsely rejected this)

cases.name: tabular-float-numeric-labels
cases.note: same values under a FLOAT label: numeric read collapses 1/1.0 into one class — both sides reject (the counterpart pin)

cases.name: tsc-ok
cases.note: 3 sequences (T=3/2/2), 2 classes, per-group monotonic INT step index — the WS1 done-contract shape (backend#1054)

cases.name: tsc-missing-sequence-col
cases.note: no sequence_id anywhere: SequenceGroupValidator rejects in-cluster; the CLI previews the fixed-column requirement (ingest.v1 conditional, Decision-2)

cases.name: tsc-null-sequence-id
cases.note: empty + 'NA' sequence ids: unassignable timestep rows — SequenceGroupValidator's null-id rule, previewed by CheckSequenceRows (both null forms via pandas NA parsing)

cases.name: tsc-none-sequence-id
cases.note: OVER-REJECT regression pin (cli#239). The literal string 'none' is a sequence id here. SequenceGroupValidator plain-reads (keep_default_na=True) and computes null as isna() | (astype(str).str.strip()==''); 'none' is NOT in pandas' STR_NA_VALUES, so it is a REAL id -> 0 nulls -> ACCEPT (2 sequences, 2 classes, per-group constant label + ascending time). Before the fix CheckSequenceRows mirrored the curated coercion.NA_SENTINELS (which DOES contain lowercase 'none') after trimming, so it saw both 'none' rows as null and REJECTED a dataset the cluster ingests fine — the dangerous over-reject direction. The Go preview now uses pandasDefaultNA + a whitespace-only clause on the RAW cell. Reverting sequenceScanFrom to naSentinels flips this to reject (mutation proof). Twin of #218's tsc-numeric-label-na-sentinel, one validator over.

cases.name: tsc-label-uniform
cases.note: single-class dataset: LabelDiversityValidator (is_classification=True composes it for TSC), previewed via the registry's IsClassification gate — not a hardcoded tabular_classification id

cases.name: tsc-label-flip
cases.note: p1 flips its label mid-sequence — LabelConstantWithinGroupValidator rejects in-cluster (one label per sequence_id), and the CLI now previews it in one grouped pass (CheckTSCGroupIntegrity → labelConstantViolation, cli#218). Closed the former documented gap.

cases.name: tsc-unsorted-timestamp
cases.note: p1's INT step index is out of order (3,1,2) — PerGroupTimeOrderedValidator rejects in-cluster (monotonic non-decreasing PER GROUP), and the CLI now previews it (CheckTSCGroupIntegrity → perGroupTimeViolation, numeric branch, cli#218). The TIMESTAMP/date branch stays a documented under-preview (matching pandas' mixed-format + locale-ambiguity parsing in Go would risk over-rejecting).

cases.name: tsc-numeric-label-na-sentinel
cases.note: OVER-REJECT regression pin (cli#218). p3's only label '#NA' is a pandas DEFAULT NA token (STR_NA_VALUES) but NOT in the curated coercion.NA_SENTINELS. LabelConstantWithinGroupValidator plain-reads the file, so pandas drops '#NA' to NaN and the label column is all-numeric -> p1's '1' and '1.0' collapse to one value (no flip) -> ACCEPT. Before the fix the CLI mirrored the coercion set, saw '#NA' as a distinct string, read the column as object, and flagged p1 as a false mid-sequence flip -> false REJECT. The Go preview now uses pandasDefaultNA for the grouped checks and accepts. (LabelDiversity reads with keep_default_na=False so '#NA' stays a literal class alongside 1/1.0/2 -> >=2 classes, still accepted.)

cases.name: objdet-ok
cases.note: object_detection happy path: paired images/annotations (valid Pascal VOC), filename-referenced manifest. Labels come from the XML, so the CSV has no label column — LabelDiversityValidator benign-skips on both sides.

cases.name: objdet-missing-annotation
cases.note: b.jpg has no b.xml: FilePairingValidator rejects in-cluster; CheckAnnotationPairing previews the image-without-annotation direction.

cases.name: objdet-orphan-annotation
cases.note: c.xml has no c.jpg: FilePairingValidator rejects orphan sidecars; CheckAnnotationPairing previews the annotation-without-image direction.

cases.name: objdet-bad-xml
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: a.xml is malformed Pascal VOC (no <size>, inverted bndbox) — PascalVOCXMLValidator rejects in-cluster, but the CLI has no XML-content mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload. Candidate for a future CheckVOCAnnotations preview.

cases.name: semseg-ok
cases.note: semantic_segmentation happy path: <stem>_mask.png pairing, 32x32 PNG masks (Mask Resolution Validator reads them), populated mask_id column. No label column — labels come from the masks; LabelDiversityValidator benign-skips on both sides.

cases.name: semseg-missing-mask
cases.note: b.jpg has no b_mask.png: FilePairingValidator (sidecar_suffix=_mask) rejects; CheckMaskPairing previews the image-without-mask direction.

cases.name: semseg-badname-mask
cases.note: b.png doesn't carry the _mask suffix: the ingestor keeps it as a non-conforming orphan AND reports b.jpg mask-less; CheckMaskPairing previews both (the #196 naming convention).

cases.name: semseg-missing-mask-id
cases.note: The manifest has no mask_id column. The CLI previews the backend#816 contract (CheckMaskIdColumn) and rejects; the pinned ingestor now includes di#358's MaskIdColumnValidator (adopted via the cli#286 pin-bump), so its preflight also rejects an undeclared mask_id. Both sides agree — reject.

cases.name: kp-ok
cases.note: keypoint_detection happy path: filename + Annotation/Visibility JSON columns, 2 keypoints in-bounds with non-degenerate bbox, visibility 0/1 with matching keys. No label column (labels come from annotations) — LabelDiversityValidator benign-skips on both sides. num_keypoints is undeclared, matching an ingest without number_of_keypoints (the count check is skipped).

cases.name: kp-res-mismatch
cases.note: 32x32 images with target [64,64]: ImageResolutionValidator rejects, and the CLI's ValidateImages previews it — pins that the image-family common mirror covers keypoint_detection, not just image_classification.

cases.name: kp-bad-annotation
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: row 1's Annotation cell isn't JSON — KeypointAnnotationValidator rejects in-cluster, but the CLI has no keypoint-annotation mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload. Candidate for a future keypoint JSON preview.

cases.name: text-clf-label-uniform
cases.note: single-class text dataset: LabelDiversityValidator (is_classification wiring) rejects; CheckLabelDiversity previews it via the registry's IsClassification gate for the text family.

cases.name: token-clf-ok
cases.note: token_classification happy path: one IOB2 tag per whitespace token (BIOLabelValidator). NOT is_classification — no diversity gate on either side (BIO tag strings aren't class labels).

cases.name: token-clf-label-missing
cases.note: no label column: BIOLabelValidator rejects the missing column in-cluster (token_classification has no LabelColumnValidator); the CLI previews it with the same CheckLabelColumn gate every supervised text task gets.

cases.name: token-clf-bad-bio
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: 'PERSON' isn't an IOB2 tag — BIOLabelValidator rejects in-cluster, but the CLI has no BIO-format mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload. Candidate for a future BIO preview.

cases.name: sentpair-ok
cases.note: sentence_pair_classification happy path: each .txt is exactly text_a<TAB>text_b (SentencePairValidator), class label in the CSV, 2 classes.

cases.name: sentpair-label-uniform
cases.note: single-class pair dataset: LabelDiversityValidator rejects (sentence_pair_classification is is_classification); CheckLabelDiversity previews via the registry gate — pins that the preview isn't hardcoded to text_classification.

cases.name: sentpair-malformed
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: a.txt has no tab (not a text_a<TAB>text_b pair) — SentencePairValidator rejects in-cluster; the CLI has no per-file structure mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload.

cases.name: mlm-ok
cases.note: masked_language_modeling happy path: self-supervised (no label column anywhere), sequences/ sidecar dir (the one text task staging from sequences/, not texts/). Pins the self-supervised dispatch: no label checks fire on either side.

cases.name: mlm-missing-files
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: every referenced sequence file is absent (an unreferenced z.txt keeps FileTypeValidator satisfied) — IngestableRecordsValidator rejects the zero-ingestable-records manifest in-cluster; the CLI has no text-family file cross-check (CrossCheckLabels is image-only), so the failure surfaces post-upload.

cases.name: clm-ok
cases.note: causal_language_modeling happy path: raw prose AND a prompt<TAB>completion file — the record_format is a convention (enforced=False), so BOTH shapes must pass on both sides (a structural reject here would be an over-reject bug).

cases.name: seq2seq-ok
cases.note: seq2seq happy path: source<TAB>target files, self-supervised (no label column), staged from texts/.

cases.name: emb-ok
cases.note: embeddings happy path: one anchor<TAB>positive pair AND one anchor<TAB>positive<TAB>negative triplet — ContrastivePairsValidator allows exactly these two shapes, so both must pass.

cases.name: emb-malformed
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: a.txt is a single field (no tab) — ContrastivePairsValidator rejects in-cluster; the CLI has no per-file structure mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload.

cases.name: tabreg-ok
cases.note: tabular_regression happy path: numeric target, inferred schema on both sides.

cases.name: tabreg-label-uniform
cases.note: OVER-REJECT guard: every label identical. Regression is NOT is_classification, so no LabelDiversityValidator fires in-cluster — and the CLI's IsClassification gate must skip too. A hardcoded category check that ran diversity here would falsely reject a valid regression dataset.

cases.name: tabreg-label-missing
cases.note: DELIBERATE divergence (the tabular-label-missing twin for regression): the ingestor's preflight has no LabelColumnValidator for tabular_regression — the failure only fires at READ time (full-schema check, after table creation, orphaning it). The CLI previews the read-time rule (cli#69).

cases.name: tsf-ok
cases.note: time_series_forecasting happy path: TIMESTAMP-typed timestamp column, ISO dates ascending and in the past, numeric features/target — passes TimeFormat/TimeOrdered/TimeBeforeToday/NumericColumns + DataValidator(schema minus timestamp).

cases.name: tsf-unsorted-time
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: rows out of chronological order — the GLOBAL TimeOrderedValidator rejects in-cluster, but the CLI only previews the grouped per-sequence variant (CheckTSCGroupIntegrity, TSC-only); TSF's whole-file ordering has no mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload.

cases.name: tsf-missing-timestamp
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: no fixed 'timestamp' column — TimeFormatValidator rejects the schema in-cluster, but the CLI's preflight has no TSF fixed-timestamp mirror (CheckSequenceSchemaColumns covers only grouped TSC columns), so the failure surfaces post-upload. Candidate: extend the fixed-column preview to TSF.

cases.name: tte-ok
cases.note: time_to_event_prediction happy path: exact 'time' column, numeric and non-negative (0.0 is legal) — TimeToEventValidator passes; inferred schema on both sides.

cases.name: tte-negative-time
cases.note: DOCUMENTED under-preview: a negative 'time' value — TimeToEventValidator rejects in-cluster, but the CLI has no TTE time-column mirror, so the failure surfaces post-upload. Candidate for a future TTE preview.
